codeReview.summary: The fix closes the prior slash-backslash open-redirect gap by hardening the shared `getSafeReturnTo()` helper that both the server-side login redirect and client-side sign-in redirect rely on. Decoding once before validation and rejecting a second-character `/` or `\` blocks the previously exploitable `'/\evil.example'` path family, while the added regression tests cover raw, encoded, mixed, and malformed variants plus a safe encoded in-app destination.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `dashboard-parity-worker` skill still appears out of sync with how scrutiny evaluates `followedProcedure`: both the original failed feature and this fix handoff reported `followedProcedure: true` even though neither transcript skeleton shows the required `ultracite` skill invocation while editing TS files.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `/home/louis/developer/faworra/faworra-new/.factory/skills/dashboard-parity-worker/SKILL.md` lists `ultracite` under Required Skills. The transcript skeleton entries for worker sessions `e41e15fd-a33c-482f-b983-ca4a1f88cd5e` and `107c39d3-ce18-4e0d-af87-218e7a69d1e4` show `dashboard-parity-worker` and `agent-browser` usage but no `"skill": "ultracite"` invocation, while both handoffs marked `skillFeedback.followedProcedure` true.

summary: Pass: the fix adequately resolves the prior blocking `return_to` sanitization failure. The shared helper now neutralizes slash-backslash and encoded external-path variants before either login-time redirect path uses them, the added tests exercise the previously missed cases, and I did not find a new blocking issue in the same scope.
